job_description: Senior ML Engineer. Build and run RAG systems in production and communicate clearly with non-ML stakeholders.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Tell me about a RAG system you built and a hard problem you ran into.

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I built a support-docs assistant. The first version retrieved by cosine similarity over naive fixed-size chunks and recall was poor for multi-part questions. I added a cross-encoder reranker and switched to semantic chunking with overlap, which moved answer accuracy on our held-out eval set from 61 to 82 percent. The tradeoff was latency, so I cached embeddings and used a small reranker to keep it under 400ms.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: How did you explain that work to non-technical stakeholders?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I framed it as 'the assistant now answers 8 in 10 questions correctly, up from 6', and showed two before-and-after examples instead of talking about rerankers.

job_description: Senior ML Engineer. Build and run RAG systems in production and communicate clearly with non-ML stakeholders.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Tell me about a RAG system you built and a hard problem you ran into.

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I used LangChain to build a chatbot over some PDFs. Retrieval sometimes returned irrelevant chunks so I increased the number of chunks returned and it got better. I used OpenAI embeddings because they're standard.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: How did you measure that it got better?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I just tried a few questions myself and it seemed to answer them okay.

job_description: Senior ML Engineer. Build and run RAG systems in production and communicate clearly with non-ML stakeholders.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Tell me about a RAG system you built and a hard problem you ran into.

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Honestly RAG is pretty straightforward once you get it. I architected a fully scalable enterprise-grade pipeline that handled everything. We used vectors and the latest models so accuracy was basically perfect. The main challenge was that other people didn't get it.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: What was the retrieval setup and how did you evaluate accuracy?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: It was all industry best practices end to end. I don't remember the exact numbers but it was definitely a huge improvement, everyone was impressed.

job_description: Senior ML Engineer. Build and run RAG systems in production and communicate clearly with non-ML stakeholders.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Tell me about a RAG system you built and a hard problem you ran into.

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Built a doc QA bot. Problem was chunking. Fixed it with semantic chunking and a reranker. Accuracy went up.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Can you say more about how you measured accuracy?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Held-out set of 50 questions, exact match on the answer span. Went from about 60 to 80 percent.

job_description: Senior ML Engineer. Build and run RAG systems in production and communicate clearly with non-ML stakeholders.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Tell me about a RAG system you built and a hard problem you ran into.

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'm really passionate about AI and I think it's going to change the world. I've watched a lot of talks about it. I'm a fast learner and a team player and I really want this job.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Sure, but can you describe a specific RAG system you built?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I haven't built one hands-on yet but I'm confident I could pick it up quickly.
